root_causes.cause: Static label misconfigured on one or more nodes (in-label/out-label mismatch)
Label conflict with dynamically allocated labels (LDP/SR range overlap)
Node added to path without label provisioned

symptoms: Traffic not forwarded on MPLS-TP path
Label not in LFIB on transit node

root_causes.cause: BFD not configured on MPLS-TP LSP
BFD session using wrong GAL (G-ACh) encapsulation
BFD interval too long for SLA requirements
BFD packet not forwarded correctly at transit nodes — GAL label (13) not processed

failure_modes.scenario: BFD for MPLS-TP not detecting fault
symptoms: LSP shows UP but traffic is black-holed
No fault detection despite physical link failure
BFD session not established on MPLS-TP tunnel

failure_modes.description: Working LSP fails but traffic does not switch to the protection LSP, causing a service outage despite 1:1 or 1+1 protection being configured

Verify protection LSP is pre-provisioned and operational

root_causes.cause: Protection LSP not pre-provisioned or itself in DOWN state
APS (Automatic Protection Switching) channel not working — RFC 6378 signaling failure
Hold-off timer delaying switch beyond acceptable time
Forced switch or lockout on protection LSP prevents automatic switch

root_causes.cause: PW Status TLV not enabled on static pseudowire
Static PW has no signaling channel for status exchange
OAM not configured on the pseudowire
Unidirectional failure not detected — no VCCV BFD

messages.description: Continuity Check / Connectivity Verification for MPLS-TP (RFC 6428). Proactive OAM for fault detection.
messages.name: BFD CC/CV
messages.when_sent: Periodically at bfd-cc-interval on each MPLS-TP LSP

messages.description: Forward defect indication (RFC 6427). Sent downstream when fault detected on the path.
messages.name: AIS (Alarm Indication Signal)
messages.when_sent: Sent by intermediate node downstream when it detects a fault on the working path

messages.description: Sent back to source MEP to indicate far-end fault detection
messages.name: RDI (Remote Defect Indication)
messages.when_sent: Sent by sink MEP toward source MEP when a defect is detected at the far end

messages.description: Variant of AIS indicating a link-layer fault on the MPLS-TP path
messages.name: LDI (Link Down Indication)
messages.when_sent: Sent when a physical link failure is detected on a link in the MPLS-TP path

messages.description: Administrative lock for maintenance (RFC 6435). Indicates LSP is locked out of service.
messages.name: LCK (Locked Signal)
messages.when_sent: Sent when an operator administratively locks an LSP for maintenance

messages.description: Pseudowire status notification (RFC 6478). Reports PW up/down without tearing down the pseudowire.
messages.name: PW Status TLV
messages.when_sent: Sent when pseudowire operational status changes (e.g., AC down, PSN fault)

purpose: MPLS Transport Profile — deterministic, connection-oriented L2 transport without IP control plane, designed for carrier Ethernet and transport networks

states.description: LSP operational, BFD session up, forwarding active
states.name: UP

states.description: LSP not operational, BFD session down or not configured
states.name: DOWN

states.description: LSP operational but OAM indicates degraded performance — high loss or delay exceeding SLA thresholds
states.name: DEGRADED

states.description: Working LSP failed, traffic switched to protection LSP (1:1 or 1+1 linear protection per RFC 6378)
states.name: PROTECTION_ACTIVE

states.description: LSP administratively locked for maintenance — LCK OAM sent, traffic blocked
states.name: LOCKED

timers.description: Continuity Check interval for MPLS-TP BFD (RFC 5884/6428)
timers.name: bfd-cc-interval
timers.on_expiry: BFD CC packet sent. 3.5x interval = fault detection time

timers.description: Delay before triggering protection switch — allows lower-layer protection to act first
timers.name: hold-off
timers.on_expiry: Protection switch triggered if fault persists

timers.description: Wait-to-Restore for linear protection in revertive mode (RFC 6378)
timers.name: wtr
timers.on_expiry: Traffic reverts from protection LSP back to restored working LSP

timers.description: Wait-to-Block for manual/forced switch commands in linear protection
timers.name: wtb
timers.on_expiry: Forced/manual switch released, normal APS operation resumes

transitions.trigger: Static LSP labels configured on all nodes, BFD session established end-to-end
BFD session failure (3x interval), AIS received, or physical link failure on LSP path
Y.1731 PM detects threshold breach — loss or delay exceeds SLA thresholds
PM metrics return to within SLA thresholds
Working LSP failure detected, linear protection switch to standby LSP (RFC 6378)
Working LSP restored, WTR timer expired, traffic reverted (revertive mode)
Administrative lock (LCK OAM sent, traffic blocked)

transport: MPLS label stack (static LSPs, no LDP/RSVP-TE signaling)

notes.note: IOS-XE supports MPLS-TP on ASR 920, ASR 903, ASR 1000. Configured under 'mpls tp'. Static LSPs with BFD CC/CV. Linear protection (1+1) supported. 'show mpls tp' hierarchy for tunnel, LSP, and link status.
IOS-XR supports MPLS-TP on ASR 9000. Static MPLS-TP tunnels with BFD-based OAM. RFC 6378 linear protection. 'show mpls tp' commands. Less commonly deployed than SR-MPLS on IOS-XR platforms.
NX-OS does not support MPLS-TP. NX-OS focuses on VXLAN/EVPN for data center overlays rather than transport network features.

MPLS-TP is a core transport feature of SAOS 8.x — designed for carrier Ethernet transport with deterministic behavior. Static LSPs, no dynamic signaling. Full OAM: BFD CC/CV, AIS, RDI, LDI, LCK. Linear protection (1:1, 1+1) per RFC 6378. PW status TLV for pseudowire monitoring. SAOS 10.x continues MPLS-TP support alongside SR-MPLS/LDP. Subject-verb CLI: 'mpls-tp show tunnel' not 'show mpls tp'. Hardware-assisted OAM on all platforms.
